human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for ambulatory-specific scheduling boundaries, source quality, order and referral handling, payer and privacy dependencies, scenario coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to ambulatory scheduling for orders, referrals, payer and self-pay dependencies, provider templates, room or equipment readiness, and patient preparation support and does not claim clinical, utilization-management, financial-approval, or final operational authority.

Authority sources cover ambulatory workflow, outpatient ordering and prior authorization, privacy-safe scheduling, self-pay estimate caveats, and HL7 scheduling artifacts, so workflow, terminology, evidence, and failure handling are materially changed by the named industry.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, capacity overload, tie-break conflict, generic scheduling rejection, clinical-triage adjacency, same-day sequencing, systems-of-record conflict, source-policy conflict, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/ambulatory-and-physician-services/ambulatory-scheduling-specialist/ so ambulatory scheduling remains separated from prior authorization approval, clinical triage, financial counseling, privacy exception, and clinic-operations authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around clinical urgency, medical necessity, estimate ownership, privacy exceptions, payer-risk acceptance, and unsupported booking commitments.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated ambulatory scheduling specialist that supports evidence-backed booking, capacity, routing, and readiness control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
